en: OK, I can do this. Deep breath.
Oh, oh, oh, OK.
Hi, morning. I've just got a very hot leg.
Oh, yo. Sorry, I'm from the agency.
Temp, not escort. Might be nine o'clock, sir. Big and busty.
Looking at you, but not listening. Right, this is your area. Do you hear what I'm saying?
Well, I do, yes, in that I'm not deaf. I'll get you a pass and a fob.
Fob? That's Sue, this is Emma, I'm Emma.
Just one thing, what does the company actually do? We provide strategic planning and data support
and recently created new media to blue-chip financial service companies across Hampshire and the Thames Valley.
So the new media boss is coming in this morning? New boss? I mean, do you hear what I'm saying?
Well, I hear you say, do you hear what I'm saying, but I don't really know what you're saying.
I totally hear what you're saying. But she only really said, do you hear what I'm saying.
No, it's fine, you know, I'll catch the lingo. You know, you're just a bit younger.
So what year were you born? 1991.
That's not what just happened.
No, you've got that wrong. Nice outfit, by the way.
Thanks, dudes, yeah, sure. The Longhurst Bailey one?
What's that? What's the company? Longhurst Bailey... Longhurst...
HE GASPS Longhurst Bailey...
Yes, yes, certainly, I'll put you through.
Just... HE HUMS
And... HE WHISTLES Longhurst Bailey...
Sorry, yes, hello, won't keep you long.
Please hold, your call is important to us. Please hold.
Do you want to play Biscuit Blizzard? Oh, God, you're so serious.
Where are the locks? Oh, I miss Stevie and the shop.
The drawing pins have been laid.
It's time for Hopper Roulette. Gladiators, mount your hoppers.
Go! HE SCREAMS
Don't need to have to pick it up and say a weird word.
Spasm. Right, listen up.
Sheath. New boss.
Right, guys, I want to get a feel for how we all tick, all right?
Let's go round the room. Names, hobbies, top strength.
You, top strength, go! Last half one.
Height! You, go.
Susan Purp, call me Sue. Sue Purp? Yes.
Sue Purp? How is that not funny?
Nothing from anyone. She's called Sue Purp.
What? What? Right, OK, that is it.
I cannot work with people who don't find that properly funny.
Have you ever walked past this meeting room and flashed during an important meeting?
If not, why not? You threw at least the odd lark,
which is why I am determined to get my beautiful shop back.
LAUGHTER Funny fact!
